Evil Martians · Ruby

Ruby & Rails LLM discoverability scorecard

Ask a frontier model to build a product app and it reaches for Python, JavaScript, or TypeScript. In our benchmark it chose Ruby on Rails 0 times out of 210 across 7 models, and 0 of 30 on the newest model (Claude Opus 4.8). Rails is fast and capable; forced to use it, the same models write idiomatic Rails (4.2/5). It is just not discoverable to the machines now writing most new code. This page measures the docs of 54 ecosystem resources and shows what the community can fix.

21/54ship an llms.txt 1/54do content negotiation 4/54serve .md docs 20/54have a sitemap 5block AI crawlers

01The scorecard

Measured over HTTP, June 2026, against each project's documentation (e.g. sorbet.org/docs, docs.avohq.io), not its landing page. Each column is a proven, checkable aspect of LLM discoverability. ✓ good, ✗ missing. “Crawlable” fetches as a Common Crawl bot to catch Cloudflare/WAF blocks. The last column samples Common Crawl coverage as pages found / sitemap total (— = not sampled, the index rate-limits bulk queries); click any column heading to sort.

Showing 54 of 54
Resource (docs) ▲ robots
allows AI ▲		 crawlable
(no WAF) ▲		 sitemap ▲ llms.txt ▲ content
negotiation ▲		 .md
routes ▲		 Common

02What will move the needle

Ordered by depth, each layer a lever on the same number. Rails is plural by design (omakase defaults plus swappable adapters and competing flavors), so the job is to strengthen the default and agree shared conventions, not to crown one winner. Each layer carries its goal as a live gauge; together they feed the final boss at the bottom.

Layer 0 — get into the corpus at all ship now

Crawlable, unblocked49/54
Sitemaps20/54
  • Unblock AI crawlers (CCBot, GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended) in robots.txt and at the WAF. The RubyEvents one-line fix alone unlocks ~15,775 pages of talks.
  • Add sitemaps, server-render, link internally, earn high-authority backlinks.
  • CC-license and transcribe conference video; Google trains Gemini on YouTube transcripts and CC-licensed talks flow into open corpora.

Layer 1 — win retrieval (content) ship now

Content negotiation1/54
.md routes4/54
llms.txt21/54
  • Serve Markdown via content negotiation and .md routes (Mime::Type.register "text/markdown", :md). A real HTTP standard agents already use, the durable bet. Ship llms.txt too, cheaply.
  • Make rdoc emit Markdown + content negotiation by default; the keystone that lifts every gem at once.
  • Publish current, specific “Rails vs X” and “build X in Rails” content with real numbers; quotations, statistics, and citations measurably raise LLM visibility.

Layer 2 — make Rails agent-operable (tools) ship now

Shared MCP interfacenot convergedAgent Skills conventionfragmented
  • Converge on a shared MCP interface (the official mcp gem) the way Active Record has one adapter interface and many drivers; the pieces exist (fast-mcp, Tidewave, rails-mcp-server).
  • Agree a shared Agent Skills convention so the many Rails skill packs interoperate.
  • Copy the proven playbook: Svelte fixed the exact “new version, AI writes old syntax” problem with llms.txt + distilled docs + an MCP server. Rails 8 has the same problem.

Layer 3 — change the training default long game

Ruby in MultiPL-EabsentRuby in Multi-SWE-benchabsentOpen idiomatic-Rails datasetnone yet
  • Get Ruby into the upstream evals labs train on. It is absent from MultiPL-E and Multi-SWE-bench (both take open contributions); adding a low-resource language to an eval measurably improves models on it (MultiPL-T, Bridge-Coder).
  • Publish an open, idiomatic-Rails instruction dataset; contribute permissively-licensed Ruby content to open corpora like Common Corpus.
  • Keep the public whichlang benchmark as the scoreboard for the final boss below, and re-run it on each new model.

★ The final boss

Frontier models pick Ruby on their own and ship working web apps with it. The one metric every layer above serves, tracked by the whichlang benchmark. The gauges below are the win condition: today they sit at zero even as the same models score well when forced, so the job is to lift them, model after model.

Chose Rails, unprompted0/210
On Opus 4.80/30
Idiomatic when forced4.2/5

03Methodology

All indicators probed over HTTP, June 2026, against each project's documentation URL. robots parsed for AI user-agents (CCBot, GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended and others) with Disallow: /; crawlability tested by fetching as a CCBot user-agent (catching Cloudflare/WAF blocks); content negotiation sent Accept: text/markdown and checked the response Content-Type; .md routes and llms.txt checked for a 200 (llms.txt at the docs host and the bare domain). The 0/210 and 0/30 default figures are from a product-task benchmark across 7 models and Opus 4.8; the 4.2/5 figure grades the same models when told to use Rails.

Evil Martians Built by Evil Martians. A living scorecard, re-run the probes to update it. Background reading: “How to make your website visible to LLMs” and “3 rules for getting AI agents to find, use, and not exploit your devtool” on the Evil Martians Chronicles.
